commit.message:
Bundle arXiv fonts at package top level, not fonts/ subdir

Path=fonts/ with a bundled fonts/ subdir failed to compile on arXiv
("font cannot be found"); bare top-level filenames resolve from the
build CWD and fall back to TeX Live if arXiv strips the binaries.
Verified end-to-end from a clean extraction on both toplevels.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -75,3 +75,7 @@
 2026-07-15 — Treat the Study A confidence field as a row-level covariate only, never as confidence in any particular field's answer, and reword its v7 label/help to say so ("How confident are you in this row overall?"). Reason: Brett reports his self-pilot confidence ratings covered the whole row and were generally not driven by the compliance judgment; the old help said "this judgment" (singular) on a five-judgment form, and the v7 reorder placed confidence directly after the global judgment, inviting misattribution. Label diagnostics corrected (column renamed self_row_confidence, README caveat); the earlier chat gloss attaching confidence to specific flips is retracted. Per-field or global-scoped confidence remains an option if a field-attributable signal is wanted, at higher burden.
 
 2026-07-15 — Scope the Study A confidence rating to the form's global judgment (task success; policy compliance) instead of the row as a whole; keys and data shape unchanged. Reason: confidence's analytical job is separating hard-item disagreement from high-confidence criterion conflict at adjudication, which needs field attribution; the v7 escape and ambiguity options already carry categorical uncertainty for every other field, so the one judgment lacking a categorical hedge gets the graded scale at no added burden. Motivated by the self-pilot finding that row-level confidence was generally unrelated to the compliance judgment. schema.md, annotation-protocol.md, and the flagship §5 adjudication sentence aligned; v5-v6 self-pilot confidence marked non-comparable on this field.
+
+2026-07-15 — arXiv package is font-free: no bundled fonts, no fontspec Path=, fonts loaded from TeX Live by bare filename (build_arxiv_bundle.sh). Reason: arXiv strips uploaded font binaries, so the bundled fonts/ + Path=fonts/ approach fails at compile with `The font "EBGaramond-Regular" cannot be found`. All three families (ebgaramond, inconsolata/InconsolataN, charissil) are stock TeX Live packages, so the preamble references them by bare filename and kpathsea resolves the TeX Live copies. Also: both toplevels (main.tex, supplement.tex) must use \input{preamble.tex}, not the repo's \input{.house-style/preamble.tex}; uploading a raw repo toplevel makes arXiv's file scan flag `.house-style/preamble.tex` missing and, because \graphicspath lives in the preamble, cascades to unresolved supplement figures. Verified end-to-end from a clean zero-font extraction: xelatex+biber×3 on both toplevels, main 18pp, supplement 10pp, 0 font errors, 0 undefined citations. Guidance for file-level arXiv replacements: raw repo files safe to upload as-is are sections/*.tex, references-local.bib, figures/*.pdf; main.tex/supplement.tex/preamble.tex must be the build-script (arXiv-flavored) versions.
+
+2026-07-15 — arXiv fonts bundled at package top level (bare filenames, no Path=), superseding the font-free build earlier the same day. Fonts included so the package is self-contained; kept at top level with bare filenames (not a fonts/ subdir with Path=fonts/, the layout that failed on arXiv). Belt-and-suspenders: bundled copies resolve from the build CWD, and if arXiv strips the binaries the same bare filenames fall back to TeX Live's ebgaramond/inconsolata/charissil. Verified from a clean extraction: main 18pp, supplement 10pp, 0 font errors, 0 undefined citations; log confirms the bundled top-level EBGaramond-Regular.otf is loaded.

@@ -6,7 +6,7 @@ OUT_DIR="$ROOT/submission/arxiv/build/source"
 BUNDLE="$ROOT/submission/arxiv/adversarial-pragmatics-arxiv-source.tar.gz"
 
 rm -rf "$OUT_DIR"
-mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR/sections" "$OUT_DIR/figures" "$OUT_DIR/fonts"
+mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR/sections" "$OUT_DIR/figures"
 
 cp "$ROOT/main.tex" "$OUT_DIR/main.tex"
 cp "$ROOT/supplement.tex" "$OUT_DIR/supplement.tex"
@@ -16,6 +16,12 @@ cp "$ROOT/references-local.bib" "$OUT_DIR/references-local.bib"
 cp "$ROOT"/sections/*.tex "$OUT_DIR/sections/"
 cp "$ROOT"/figures/*.pdf "$OUT_DIR/figures/"
 
+# Bundle the fonts at the package top level (NOT a fonts/ subdir) and reference
+# them by bare filename with no Path= in the preamble. Top-level + bare filename
+# means kpathsea finds the bundled copy in the build CWD; and if arXiv strips the
+# uploaded font binaries, the same bare filenames still resolve against TeX Live's
+# ebgaramond / inconsolata / charissil. Do not reintroduce Path=fonts/: a bundled
+# fonts/ subdir plus Path=fonts/ is what failed on arXiv.
 for font in \
   EBGaramond-Regular.otf \
   EBGaramond-Italic.otf \
@@ -30,21 +36,21 @@ for font in \
 do
   font_path="$(kpsewhich "$font" || true)"
   if [[ -z "$font_path" ]]; then
-    echo "Missing font $font; cannot build self-contained arXiv bundle." >&2
+    echo "Missing font $font; cannot build self-contained bundle." >&2
     exit 1
   fi
-  cp "$font_path" "$OUT_DIR/fonts/$font"
+  cp "$font_path" "$OUT_DIR/$font"
 done
 
 perl -0pi -e 's/\\input\{\.house-style\/preamble\.tex\}/\\input\{preamble.tex\}/g' \
   "$OUT_DIR/main.tex" "$OUT_DIR/supplement.tex"
-perl -0pi -e 's/\\setmainfont\{EB Garamond\}\[\n  Numbers=OldStyle,\n  Ligatures=TeX,\n  BoldFont=\{EB Garamond\},\n\]/\\setmainfont{EBGaramond-Regular.otf}[\n  Path=fonts\/,\n  Numbers=OldStyle,\n  Ligatures=TeX,\n  ItalicFont=EBGaramond-Italic.otf,\n  BoldFont=EBGaramond-Bold.otf,\n  BoldItalicFont=EBGaramond-BoldItalic.otf,\n]/g' \
+perl -0pi -e 's/\\setmainfont\{EB Garamond\}\[\n  Numbers=OldStyle,\n  Ligatures=TeX,\n  BoldFont=\{EB Garamond\},\n\]/\\setmainfont{EBGaramond-Regular.otf}[\n  Numbers=OldStyle,\n  Ligatures=TeX,\n  ItalicFont=EBGaramond-Italic.otf,\n  BoldFont=EBGaramond-Bold.otf,\n  BoldItalicFont=EBGaramond-BoldItalic.otf,\n]/g' \
   "$OUT_DIR/preamble.tex"
-perl -0pi -e 's/\\newfontfamily\\ipafont\{Charis SIL\}/\\newfontfamily\\ipafont{CharisSIL-Regular.ttf}[\n  Path=fonts\/,\n  ItalicFont=CharisSIL-Italic.ttf,\n  BoldFont=CharisSIL-Bold.ttf,\n  BoldItalicFont=CharisSIL-BoldItalic.ttf,\n]/g' \
+perl -0pi -e 's/\\newfontfamily\\ipafont\{Charis SIL\}/\\newfontfamily\\ipafont{CharisSIL-Regular.ttf}[\n  ItalicFont=CharisSIL-Italic.ttf,\n  BoldFont=CharisSIL-Bold.ttf,\n  BoldItalicFont=CharisSIL-BoldItalic.ttf,\n]/g' \
   "$OUT_DIR/preamble.tex"
 perl -0pi -e 's/% CJK fallback font \(Japanese \/ Chinese \/ Korean\)\n\\newfontfamily\\cjkfont\{Hiragino Mincho ProN\}\n\\newcommand\{\\cjk\}\[1\]\{\{\\cjkfont #1\}\}/% CJK fallback disabled for the arXiv package; this submission contains no CJK text.\n\\newcommand{\\cjk}[1]{#1}/g' \
   "$OUT_DIR/preamble.tex"
-perl -0pi -e 's/\\setmonofont\{Inconsolata\}\[Scale=MatchLowercase\]/\\setmonofont{InconsolataN-Regular.otf}[\n  Path=fonts\/,\n  Scale=MatchLowercase,\n  BoldFont=InconsolataN-Bold.otf,\n]/g' \
+perl -0pi -e 's/\\setmonofont\{Inconsolata\}\[Scale=MatchLowercase\]/\\setmonofont{InconsolataN-Regular.otf}[\n  Scale=MatchLowercase,\n  BoldFont=InconsolataN-Bold.otf,\n]/g' \
   "$OUT_DIR/preamble.tex"
 perl -0pi -e 's/\\IfFileExists\{references-standalone\.bib\}\s*\n\s*\{\\addbibresource\{references-standalone\.bib\}\}\s*\n\s*\{\\addbibresource\{references\.bib\}\\IfFileExists\{references-local\.bib\}\{\\addbibresource\{references-local\.bib\}\}\{\}\}/% Bibliography resources are declared explicitly in each arXiv top-level TeX file./g' \
   "$OUT_DIR/preamble.tex"

@@ -17,7 +17,9 @@ The generated archive is:
 submission/arxiv/adversarial-pragmatics-arxiv-source.tar.gz
 ```
 
-The bundle is generated from tracked source files. It flattens the house-style preamble to `preamble.tex`, keeps section files under `sections/`, includes the PDF figures used by the paper, includes both `.bib` sources, bundles the open font files needed by XeLaTeX, and writes a `00README.json` that tells arXiv to compile `main.tex` and `supplement.tex` as XeLaTeX top-level files in that order using TeX Live 2025. The generated archive and build directory are intentionally ignored by git.
+The bundle is generated from tracked source files. It flattens the house-style preamble to `preamble.tex` (and rewrites both toplevels to `\input{preamble.tex}`, never `.house-style/`), keeps section files under `sections/`, includes the PDF figures used by the paper, includes both `.bib` sources, and writes a `00README.json` that tells arXiv to compile `main.tex` and `supplement.tex` as XeLaTeX top-level files in that order using TeX Live 2025. The generated archive and build directory are intentionally ignored by git.
+
+Fonts are bundled at the package top level (not a `fonts/` subdir) and referenced by bare filename with no `Path=`. Top-level + bare filename means kpathsea finds the bundled copy in the build directory; if arXiv strips the uploaded font binaries, the same bare filenames still resolve against TeX Live's `ebgaramond` / `inconsolata` / `charissil`. Do NOT reintroduce a `fonts/` subdir with `Path=fonts/`: that combination is what failed on arXiv (`The font "EBGaramond-Regular" cannot be found`). Verified: both toplevels build from a clean extraction (main 18pp, supplement 10pp, no font errors, no undefined citations).
 
 Upload notes:
